nodes.label: Through-the-Wall Detection of Stationary Human Targets Using Doppler Radar (Narayanan et al., PIER-B 2010)
nodes.source_location: Progress In Electromagnetics Research B, Vol. 20, 147-166, 2010
nodes.author: R. M. Narayanan, M. C. Shastry, P.-H. Chen, M. Levi

nodes.label: Doppler Radar Through-Wall Human Detection
nodes.rationale: Detecting Doppler signatures from breathing and limb micro-movements allows identification of stationary humans behind walls using a 750 MHz Doppler radar; EMD algorithm extracts transient non-stationary features.

nodes.label: Empirical Mode Decomposition (EMD) for Doppler Feature Extraction
nodes.rationale: EMD adaptively decomposes non-stationary Doppler signals into intrinsic mode functions without requiring a priori basis, enabling extraction of irregular human motion signatures.
